title: monadic-conversation
description: Standardized conversation data schema developed by the Monadic Chat project. Supports 2-party (user/assistant), multi-party (3+), and monologue (e.g., TED Talks) conversations. The 'monadic-' prefix is the schema family's proper noun, reflecting its lineage from state-monadic context threading in functional programming; it does not assert categorical monad properties.

format_version.description: Schema format version. Currently fixed at '1.0'.

conversation_id.description: Unique identifier for the conversation. UUID v4 recommended.

source.description: Origin of the conversation data. Examples: 'monadic-chat', 'ted-talk', 'imported-chatml', 'imported-anthropic', 'plain-text'.

external_id.description: External identifier when imported from another corpus (e.g., TED talk ID).

license.description: License identifier for the content. Free-form string. Examples: 'CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0', 'CC-BY-4.0', 'private', 'proprietary'. Required for accountability and academic reuse.

retrieved_at.description: When the conversation was ingested into the Library.

duration_seconds.description: Total duration in seconds (for time-based content like talks).

annotations.description: Linguistic / discourse annotations layered on top of the conversation. Empty array in v1.0 baseline; populated by Phase 2+ tools.

role.description: Protocol-level functional category. 'human' = real speaker / user / participant; 'assistant' = LLM or AI agent; 'system' = system instruction author; 'narrator' = single-speaker monologue narrator; 'other' = anything else (e.g., non-LLM machine, animal vocalization).

description.description: Free-form descriptive label for the participant's role in the conversation (e.g., 'mother', 'target_child', 'audience_member', 'investigator', 'facilitator'). Inspired by CHILDES CHAT format participant codes — open vocabulary, not enumerated. Empty or omitted is valid for simple 2-party chats.

metadata.description: Free-form participant metadata (e.g., age, sex, native_language, demographic info). Use for research applications that need richer participant profiles.

id.description: Conversation-unique message identifier. Convention: 'msg-NNNN' or UUID.

text.description: Current/latest text. If the message has been edited, prior versions live in metadata.edit_history.

timestamp.description: Wall-clock time of the message (e.g., when the LLM responded, or when the user posted). Both timestamp and timing may be present, or either alone.

timing.description: Time-based positioning within content (used for talk transcripts, audio-tracked conversations).

message_metadata.description: Per-message metadata. Open shape: well-known fields are documented here, but importers / Monadic Chat itself may add app-specific fields (e.g., tts_instructions, mid, images).

provider.description: LLM provider when this message is an assistant response (e.g., 'openai', 'anthropic', 'gemini').

edited.description: True if this message was edited after initial creation.

edit_history.description: Prior versions in chronological order (oldest first). The current 'text' field is the latest version, not duplicated here.

at.description: When the prior version was replaced.

text.description: The text content at that point in history.

reason.description: user_edit = user manually edited; regeneration = re-generated by LLM; tool_correction = automatic tool/agent fixup; system_revision = system-level revision.

tools.description: Tool calls associated with this message (LLM-side calls). Free-shape per provider.

monadic_state.description: Monadic Chat's state JSON at the time of this message. External imports may omit this entirely.

conversation_context.description: ContextExtractorAgent-managed common context. Default fields: topics / people / notes; apps may extend with custom schema fields.

app_specific.description: Per-app freely-shaped state (e.g., NovelWriter characters, MathTutor solved_problems). No schema constraint at v1.0.

label.description: Annotation label (controlled vocabulary depends on tier).

anchor_conversation.description: Marker for conversation-level annotations (no specific message reference).
